title: Change your email address – Update your personal information with the CRA - Canada.ca
content:
Update your personal information with the CRA

## Update your personal information with the CRA - Change your email address
My Account users are required to have an email address on file with the CRA to help protect their accounts from fraudulent activity. You can only have one email address on file at a time in My Account.
The email address that you provide will be used to tell you about:
- important changes made on your account
- new mail to view in My Account

## Ways to update your email address
Choose an option to learn how to make changes to your account.
Processing time: Immediate
Change your email address online using your CRA account.
- Sign in to your CRA account
- Select your Individual account to access My Account
- Scroll to Notification preference

## If you cannot sign in to your CRA account
If your phone number has changed and you cannot receive your multi-factor authentication (MFA) passcode to sign in, you will have to update your information by phone .
For more help with My Account, go to: Help with using your CRA account

## Make a change for someone else
To change an email address online for someone else, including a spouse or family member, you must be their legal representative and use Represent a Client in your CRA account.
Processing time: Immediate
- Social insurance number (SIN)
- Full name and date of birth
- Assessed tax return, notice of assessment or reassessment, or other tax document
You can also be signed in to your CRA account to access your tax information.

## Make a change for someone else
You can only call on behalf of someone else in the following 2 situations:
- The person is present on the call to verify their identity in English or French and provides verbal authorization for you to speak for them
The person is present on the call to verify their identity in English or French and provides verbal authorization for you to speak for them
- You are the person's legal representative with full legal authority to act as them For details: Authorize a representative
You are the person's legal representative with full legal authority to act as them
For details: Authorize a representative
The CRA only accepts collect calls made through a telephone operator. After your call is accepted by an automated response, you may hear a beep and notice a normal connection delay. This service operates in Eastern Standard Time.
Teletypewriter (TTY):
1-800-665-0354 (opens up tty application) 1-800-665-0354
Canada VRS (Video Relay Service):
Register with Canada VRS to download the app and call the VRS line
If you use any other operator-assisted relay service, call our regular telephone numbers on this page instead of the TTY or Canada VRS numbers.
- If you file electronically , at the same date and time your return is processed
- If you file a paper return , approximately 12 weeks after receiving your return
You can change your email address when filing your return using one of the following methods:
- On your paper tax return
- Filing electronically using NETFILE
- Filing electronically through a tax preparer or authorized service provider using EFILE
However, you should not wait for tax filing season to update your information. In some cases, identification changes could have a direct impact on your eligibility for benefits.

## Updating your email address with other government departments
Updating your email address with the CRA does not update your information with other government departments.
To update your email address or other personal information, including direct deposit, with other government organizations, you will need to contact each one directly .
Contact another Government of Canada department or agency

## Updating other information
Change other personal details with the CRA, including your home or mailing address, or phone number.
Update your personal information with the CRA

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
